question_text: Two planets have the same mass but Planet B has twice the radius of Planet A. How does the gravitational field strength on Planet B compare to Planet A?

options.label: A
options.text: Planet B has the same gravitational field strength as Planet A

options.label: B
options.text: Planet B has half the gravitational field strength of Planet A

options.label: C
options.text: Planet B has one-quarter the gravitational field strength of Planet A

options.label: D
options.text: Planet B has four times the gravitational field strength of Planet A

explanation: Gravitational field strength is inversely proportional to the square of the distance from the centre. If the radius (and thus surface distance) doubles, the gravitational field strength becomes 1/(2²) = 1/4 of the original value. Planet B's surface is twice as far from the centre, so its field strength is one-quarter that of Planet A.
